Okay, so I want to talk about creating a culture of empowerment in your business. And this is probably the number one concept that I, or concept or, yeah, I guess concept that I focused on when building our company. In that I wanted people to feel empowered, I wanted people to like where they worked, I wanted people to feel valued. And so I really highly suggest you do the same. And this is how we do it, right? So the way I view it is that culture trump strategy. Why on earth would I say that when strategy is how you survive in a marketplace? Because if you hire and develop the right people, then they will build the strategy for you. And end of the day, what we are teaching you how to do is how to not be the only one carrying the business. We want you to use your team. You don't hire a team and pay people to be more stressed. You hire a team and pay people so they can help build the business with you, right? And so I really believe that if you produce the right culture, it will produce the people that can build that strategy for you, right? And honestly, if you don't let other people lead, then you become the sole cap to your business. And this is something that's really hard for people to understand. I know it's very hard for people in fitness, particularly actually, because what I have come to realize over the years is that fitness enthusiasts, people who start gyms, people who start personal training industry jobs, whatever it may be, you do it because you want to help people and you're passionate about it, right? Not because you want to make shit tons of money. And so the issue that arises is that you become the cap to your business because you believe that you are the only person that could ever do it, the only person that cares as much about the clients, the only person that will ever be able to do your job that well, right? And that's what happens. And then it prevents you from actually growing and scaling your team, right? And so what you need to switch your mindset to is instead of being the almighty, powerful to having an empowered team, okay? So a universally acknowledged example of this would be Jesus, okay? Why is Jesus an example I'm using right now? Because what he did was he identified 12 men with little experience. He invested his entire life into them, right? He empowered them. He spent three whole years with them. And then he said, he told them to go change the world and make disciples of all nations, okay? He didn't give them a task list to check down one through 30. He invested lots of time training, developing, being around them before he told them what to do. And then telling them what to do, he didn't have to be insanely specific. He had to tell them one task, right? And so really what I want to, the point I would like to get across with this is that the identities, that how to identify the two currencies of empowerment, right? Which are clarity and trust. And so Jesus, clarity, go make disciples of all nations, trust came from the fact that he spent so much time training and developing these people, right? So how do we apply and measure these currencies? How do we actually put these into actions? What does it look like within your business, okay? One is you communicate with clarity and then two is that you extend trust, okay? Communicate with clarity. That means you identify the what, what? Go and make disciples. Where? All nations. When? Now and for the rest of life. You could add on the why and the how in there and then you'd have an entire goal built up. But you get my point, you need to be very clear with how you're communicating to people. And what about the how? How? Well, you extend trust and let the person figure out the how, okay? And so I know a lot of what you guys are thinking right now is you're like, Leila, how do I know if I can trust somebody? Okay? Well, the best way to figure out if you can trust someone is to just trust them. And here's the truth. Is that four out of five times, it's going to go great. And then one out of five times, you're going to get screwed. And as a business owner, you have to accept that. That is reality. I know that out of 10 customer service firms we hire, we're going to put it in the business and we're going to probably have to let go of three of them. That's reality. Okay? You have to trust people anyways. You have to give them a chance. Otherwise, if you dwell on it and you're just, oh, I just can't trust this person or I just can't believe they messed up. It's like, get over it. It's business. It's life. People are human. They make mistakes. You make mistakes all the time. Like that's life. Okay? So just trust them. That's how you found out. If you should trust someone, you trust them first. Okay? You need to give them the freedom and you need to give them the opportunity. You invest in them and then you trust them. Okay? So that means you invest in the hiring process. You make sure that you have picked the right person for the job and then you invest in their training, meaning they can still be qualified for the job, but they might not have context as to how to do that job because you have not spent enough time with them. Therefore, they don't know how to prioritize. They don't know how to make good decisions because you haven't invested enough time in them from the get-go. Right? So once you have invested in the hiring process and invested in the person, that is when you trust them to do the mission. Right? And then they execute the how. Okay. But what is clarity without trust and what is trust without clarity? Okay? Let me tell you what happens. Clarity without trust produces fear and inaction. Okay? That's when somebody knows what to do but doesn't believe that you, the leader, trust them to do it and so they freeze. Okay? That's like a very classic example would be if you pull someone aside and you're like, I can't believe you did this. You do this shit again. You pull this off again. You're out of gear. Right? Talking to someone like that. They understand exactly what you don't want them to do and they understand exactly what you want them to do and they're freaked the fuck out and so they're not going to move. Okay? And so that's why it's very important that you pay attention to how you speak to people, how you present yourself to people. You cannot run your gym over with fear. Like you cannot let people fear you. It has to be that you are inspiring people and empowering people and that is how you motivate people to do things. Okay? Second is trust without clarity. Trust without clarity produces work without direction. I've seen this personally many times. Okay? It leads to busy work because they have no idea of the order of importance of the things on their plate or that there is even a goal. Right? And so what happens a lot of times is that you go and you'll dig into a certain part of your business and you're like, all right, you know, maybe show me what you're doing every day and you're like, what's that project? And they're like, well, I thought this would be really cool if we could do and you're like sitting there like, that would be really cool. Like fuck. You need to make more sales. Like how is that a priority? The reason that it's not a priority to them is because you didn't make yourself clear about what was. Okay? And so they don't have clear enough goals. They don't have clear enough guidelines. And so they're working on things that they think will add value because you've not told them what will add the most value. Okay? So all sums back to you as the leader. Clarity is going to ensure that your team does the work that is aligned with your goals and mission. Okay? So it's so important that you repeat yourself, repeat yourself and repeat yourself about what your goals are, what your mission is and what your values are. Okay? Because three out of four times, Sally isn't going to hear what you're saying because Sally is thinking about her boyfriend back home. Sally is thinking about the date she's going on tonight. She's thinking about what shoes she's going to wear. She's not thinking about the fuck, like the stuff that you're talking about with how to hit sales goals, why the numbers are low, et cetera, et cetera. Right? Or she's not thinking about like the big mission and vision that you guys have the business like her mind is somewhere else. Okay? So repeat yourself and be very clear every time you do it. And once you do this, they'll know how to contribute to the big picture goal and how necessary their work is. A lot of people will feel that their work is unimportant unless you show them how it ties in with the entire big picture. What most business owners have that a lot of employees lack is perspective of the organization as a whole. And so you see the holistic view of it. You see how everything ties in together. They don't see that unless you show it to them repeatedly, day in and day out. Okay? Trust is a necessary net that results in risk taking. Okay? Because if you believe in the person, then they believe that they can try and they have the freedom to create, to innovate, to try and to fail. If you do not believe in the person, then they are too scared to do any of that. And so if you want your team to be doing things new, if you want all the things you're learning in gym legacy to come to life, then you have to be able to extend trust and know that they're going to mess up. I mean, think about it like this, like really think about it like this. How many times do you mess up in the business? How many times do you make mistakes? Why is it that you expect anything differently of your team if you're asking them to take risks and make new things and try new stuff? You can't expect differently. You have to accept the fact that it will cost you to implement new things. It will cost you to try new ideas. And that is just part of being in business. Okay? So then the question becomes, what if I don't trust the people that I work? And this is the statement I would like to leave you with, which is either then you are the wrong, you have the wrong team, or you are the wrong leader, but either way at the end of the day, that is up to you to solve that problem. And so this is something I see a lot. When someone says, I just don't think I've got the right team. And I would like to ask you to turn around and say, is it that you don't have the right team or is that you are not the right leader? Okay. So I will leave you with that.
